name: Snowflake
tagline: Snowflake lets you interactively create recursive-descent parsers.
body:
Snowflake is licensed under the [Apache 2.0](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html) licence.

Snowflake is actually two projects in one, each of which are described below. 

**Part I:** The Library

The library facilitates the creation of [PEG](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parsing_expression_grammar)-based recursive-descent parsers at runtime.

Quick Links:
* [Download the library as a precompiled jar file.](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/snowflake.jar)
* [Browse the library's source-code.](https://github.com/Mackenzie-High/snowflake/tree/master/snowflake-library)
* [View the Javadoc documentation for the library.](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/javadoc/snowflake-library/index.html)
* [View unit-test coverage statistics.](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/latest-test-coverage-statistics.jpeg)

**Part II:** The Graphical Grammar Editor (a.k.a. The Snowflake GUI)

The grammar editor provides you the ability to interactively develop [PEG](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parsing_expression_grammar) grammars. Specifically, upon entering a grammar and some input, you can immediately view the resulting parse-tree in browsable form.

Quick Links:
* [Quick Tutorial (Only 10 Slides)](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/Snowflake-GUI-Getting-Started.ppt)
* [Cheat Sheet](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/Snowflake-GUI-Cheat-Sheet.pdf)
* [Download the grammar editor as a single precompiled ready-to-run jar file.](http://Mackenzie-High.github.io/snowflake/other/snowflake-gui.jar)

**Part III:** Downloadable Grammars
* [Simple Phone Book] (http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/phonebook.snow)
* [Prefix Notation Calculator](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/prefix-calculator.snow)
* [Infix Notation Calculator](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/infix-calculator.snow)

**Part IV:** Downloadable Projects - These projects include executable example programs. 
* [Simple Phone Book] (http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/phonebook-example.zip)
* [Prefix Notation Calculator](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/prefix-calculator-example.zip)
* [Infix Notation Calculator](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/infix-calculator-example.zip)

**Part V:** Here are some other resources and links that may help you.
* [Wikipedia: Parsing Expression Grammars](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parsing_expression_grammar)
* [The Packrat Parsing and Parsing Expression Grammars Page](http://bford.info/packrat/)
* [Parsing Expression Grammars: An Example Driven Introduction](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/Grammars.pdf)
* [The Grammar of Snowflake Grammars](https://github.com/Mackenzie-High/snowflake/blob/master/snowflake-gui/src/high/mackenzie/snowflake/parsergen/GrammarOfGrammars.txt)
